• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

EXPORTS North Atlantic (PAP) Epoch 1 vs Epoch 3 Comparison

Connor Shea

July 29, 2025

Zooplankton were collected via paired day-night MOCNESS tows (0-1000\(\mathrm{\ m}\)) over the course of a month-long occupation of the Porcupine Abyssal Plane observatory in the North Atlantic during May of 2021. Samples were collected on May 6 (Epoch 1), May 17 (Epoch 2), and May 26 (Epoch 3). Zooplankton were size fractionated following collection and frozen for isotopic analysis. After transport to Hawaii, samples were vacuum dried, ground, and weighed. Amino acids were extracted, purified, and derivitized for AA-CSIA from the Epoch 1 and Epoch 3 collections.

In this document we will be visualizing trends in zooplankton amino acid \(\mathrm{\delta^{15}\text{N}}\) data relative to those observed in particles, specifically focusing on comparing zooplankton collected in Epochs 1 and 3.

Setup

The first chunk identifies tracers to be included in the model and how to treat them.


# # Which amino acid δ15N values should be included in the model? (should be consistent with column names in data file)
# # Specify the column names of all amino acid d15N values that will be included in the model and their standard deviations
# # Tracers which will see the data and be used to fit the mixing model will be designated further below
# # To include all amino acid δ15N values...
# tracers_d15N <- c("d15NGlx","d15NAsx","d15NAla","d15NIle","d15NLeu","d15NPro","d15NVal","d15NGly","d15NSer","d15NPhe","d15NLys","d15NThr")
# SDtracers_d15N <- c("SDd15NGlx","SDd15NAsx","SDd15NAla","SDd15NIle","SDd15NLeu","SDd15NPro","SDd15NVal","SDd15NGly","SDd15NSer","SDd15NPhe","SDd15NLys","SDd15NThr")

## SPECIFYING TDFS ##

## What TDFs and SDs should be used to describe amino acid δ15N fractionation in
## metazoans?
## Note that these TDFs should NOT be normalized to any source amino acid.
TDF_meta <- data.frame("d15NAla" = 6.3, "SDd15NAla" = 2.6,
                       "d15NGly" = 2.9, "SDd15NGly" = 3.1,
                       "d15NThr" =-4.9, "SDd15NThr" = 3.5,
                       "d15NSer" = 2.6, "SDd15NSer" = 3.2,
                       "d15NVal" = 4.4, "SDd15NVal" = 2.6,
                       "d15NLeu" = 5.6, "SDd15NLeu" = 2.4,
                       "d15NIle" = 5.5, "SDd15NIle" = 2.4,
                       "d15NPro" = 5.8, "SDd15NPro" = 1.7,
                       "d15NAsx" = 5.7, "SDd15NAsx" = 1.9,
                       "d15NMet" = 1.6, "SDd15NMet" = 2.6,
                       "d15NGlx" = 8.0, "SDd15NGlx" = 1.7,
                       "d15NPhe" = 0.3, "SDd15NPhe" = 0.5,
                       "d15NTyr" = NA , "SDd15NTyr" = NA,
                       "d15NLys" = 1.2, "SDd15NLys" = 1.2)

## What TDFs and SDs should be used to describe amino acid δ15N fractionation in
## protozoans?
# Note that these TDFs should NOT be normalized to any source amino acid.
TDF_proto = data.frame("d15NAla" = 6.3, "SDd15NAla" = 2.6,
                       "d15NGly" = 2.9, "SDd15NGly" = 3.1,
                       "d15NThr" =-2.1, "SDd15NThr" = 1.0,
                       "d15NSer" = 2.6, "SDd15NSer" = 3.2,
                       "d15NVal" = 0.7, "SDd15NVal" = 1.6,
                       "d15NLeu" = 1.4, "SDd15NLeu" = 0.6,
                       "d15NIle" =-0.5, "SDd15NIle" = 2.7,
                       "d15NPro" = 5.8, "SDd15NPro" = 1.7,
                       "d15NAsx" = 0.8, "SDd15NAsx" = 1.4,
                       "d15NMet" = NA , "SDd15NMet" = NA ,
                       "d15NGlx" = 0.5, "SDd15NGlx" = 1.0,
                       "d15NPhe" = 0.3, "SDd15NPhe" = 0.5,
                       "d15NTyr" = NA , "SDd15NTyr" = NA,
                       "d15NLys" = 1.2, "SDd15NLys" = 1.2)
## Which parameters will we normally use to calculate TP?
TP.parms = data.frame("AAs"   = c("glx-phe", "ala-phe", "thr-phe", "trp-src"),
                      "TDF"   = c(7.6      , 5.1      ,-5.8      , 6.3),
                      "SDTDF" = c(1.2      , 2.1      , 3.2      , 0.9),
                      "beta"  = c(3.4      , 3.2      ,-2.4      , 2.2),
                      "SDbeta"= c(0.9      , 1.2      , 0.4      , 0.7)
                      )

# Last we'll define some generic groups of AAs that may be useful later on.
# Update to reflect the column names in your data
# all amino acids
allAA = c("Ala", "Gly", "Thr", "Ser", "Val", "Leu", "Ile", "Pro", "Asx", "Met", "Glx", "Phe", "Tyr", "Lys")
# columns with SD
SDallAA <- c("SDAla", "SDGly", "SDThr", "SDSer", "SDVal", "SDLeu", "SDIle", "SDPro", "SDAsx", "SDMet", "SDGlx", "SDPhe", "SDTyr", "SDLys")
# columns with sd
sdallAA <- c("sdAla", "sdGly", "sdThr", "sdSer", "sdVal", "sdLeu", "sdIle", "sdPro", "sdAsx", "sdMet", "sdGlx", "sdPhe", "sdTyr", "sdLys")
# source amino acids
srcAA = c("Gly","Ser","Phe","Lys") # ALL SRC AA: c("Phe","Met","Tyr","Gly","Ser","Lys")
# columns with SD
SDsrcAA = c("SDGly","SDSer","SDPhe","SDLys")
# columns with sd
sdsrcAA = c("sdGly","sdSer","sdPhe","sdLys")
# ALL SRC AA: c("SDPhe","SDMet","SDTyr","SDGly","SDSer","SDLys")
# trophic amino acids
trAA = c("Glx", "Asx", "Ala", "Ile", "Leu", "Pro", "Val","Thr")
# columns with SD
SDtrAA = c("SDGlx", "SDAsx", "SDAla", "SDIle", "SDLeu", "SDPro", "SDVal","SDThr")
# columns with sd
sdtrAA = c("sdGlx", "sdAsx", "sdAla", "sdIle", "sdLeu", "sdPro", "sdVal","sdThr")

Next let’s define any additional functions we will need to analyze this Data.


# defining function to propagate uncertainty for mean calculations
SDmean <- function(data, na.rm = FALSE)
{
  if (prod(!is.na(data)) == 1) {
    squareds = data^2
    sumofsquareds = sum(squareds)
    root = sqrt(sumofsquareds)
    SD = root/length(as.double(data))
    return(SD)
  } else { if (na.rm == TRUE) {
    data = na.omit(data)
    squareds = data^2
    sumofsquareds = sum(squareds)
    root = sqrt(sumofsquareds)
    SD = root/length(data)
    return(SD)
  } else {
    return(NA)
  }
  }
}

# defining a function to propagate error through summing calculations
SDsum <- function(data)
{
   if (is.numeric(data) == TRUE) {
        squareds = data^2
        sumofsquareds = sum(squareds)
        root = sqrt(sumofsquareds)
        SD = root
        return(SD)
    } else {
        return(NA)
    }
}

Next we will need to import data for zooplankton and particles.


## Columns containing amino acid δ-values and the associated standard 
## deviations should match those defined in the chunk above.

# Indicate the name of the column describing the organic matter groups
Source_Variable <- "Group"
# Indicate the names representing each possible source of organic matter in the
# order in which you would like the referenced
Sources <- c("Surface","Large","Small","Submicron")

# List the name of any additional variables that should be stored for analysis
Additional_variables <- c("Location","Epoch","Event","Size","Depth","Type")

variables <- c(Source_Variable, Additional_variables)

## IMPORTING DATA ##
# Define the location of .xlsx file, and sheet name if multiple sheets are 
# present. If additional data beyond organic matter sources is present in this 
# file that is ok. We will subset the data below
Data.all <- read_excel("Data/AA-CSIA_PAP.xlsx", 
    sheet = "Data")
# Defining the preferred order in which to reference organic matter sources
Data.all[[Source_Variable]] <- factor(Data.all[[Source_Variable]], levels = c(Sources,"Trap","Mixed","Taxa","Migrant","Resident"))
Data.all$Sizecat <- factor(Data.all$Sizecat, 
                           levels = c(
                             # Particles
                             "Surface", "Large", "Small", "Submicron",  "Mixed", "Trap",
                             # Carnivorous zooplankton
                             "Chaetognath", "Stylocheiron", "Gaetanus", "Hyperiid",
                             # Detritivorous zooplankton
                             "Oncaeid", "Oithonid",
                             # Vertically migrating zooplenkton
                             "Metridia", "Pleuromamma robusta", "Pleuromamma", "Undeuchaeta plumosa", "Hyperiidae"))
# Defining the preferred order in which to reference size classes
Data.all$Size <- factor(Data.all$Size, 
                        levels = c(
                          #Particle sizes
                          "0.3-1 μm", "1-5 μm", "1.0-6.0 μm", "0.8-51 μm", "6.0-51 μm", 
                          ">51 μm", "51-335 μm", ">335  μm", "Trap", 
                          # Zooplankton sizes
                          "0.2-0.5 mm", "0.5-1.0 mm", "1-2 mm", "2-5 mm", ">5 mm", "Taxa"
                          ))

## FILTERING DATA ##
# In the event that not all data in data file pertains to sources of organic
# matter, or should be otherwise excluded from analysis, a subset of the data
# can be taken at this point.
# Data.sources_orig <- subset(Data_all,
#                        (Depth >= 200 &
#                           (Group == "Large" | Group == "Small" | Group == "Submicron")) |
#                          Group == "Surface")
## DEALING WITH GAPS IN THE DATA ##

# Should representative samples of organic matter end members be simulated
# from available data? (useful if a large number of samples have only δ13C or 
# δ15N data). If FALSE, samples without values for all tracers will be excluded.
simulate_rep <- FALSE# SHOULD CHOOSE FALSE BY DEFAULT

## if we chose not to simulate representative samples...
if (simulate_rep == FALSE) { 
  Data_sources <- na.omit(Data_sources_orig[c(variables,tracers,SDtracers)]) # all samples containing NAs will be removed from data
  ## we will also find the mean tracer value for each source group
  src.mn <- aggregate(Data_sources_orig[tracers], # aggregate source data
                      by=list(Group = Data_sources_orig[[Source_Variable]]), # by organic matter source group
                      FUN = mean, na.rm=TRUE)[-1] # taking a mean
  
## if we chose to simulate representative samples...
} else {
  ## first we will find the mean tracer value for each source group
  ## as well as the mean SD within each source group
  src.mn <- aggregate(Data_sources_orig[c(tracers,SDtracers)], # aggregate source data
                      by=list(Group = Data_sources_orig[[Source_Variable]]), # by organic matter source group
                      FUN = mean, na.rm=TRUE)[-1] # taking a mean
  ## Next we will find the most conservative estimate of tracer variance within 
  ## each source group
  # we will calculate the standard deviation within each population
  src.SD.pop <- aggregate(Data_sources_orig[tracers], # aggregate source data
                          by=list(Group = Data_sources_orig[[Source_Variable]]), # by organic matter source group
                          FUN = sd, na.rm=TRUE)[-1] # calculating SD within the population
  colnames(src.SD.pop) <- c(SDtracers)
  # we will also propagate analytical uncertainty through the averaging equation
  src.SD.prop <- aggregate(Data_sources_orig[SDtracers], # aggregate source data SDs
                           by=list(Group = Data_sources_orig[[Source_Variable]]), # by organic matter source group
                           FUN = SDmean, na.rm=TRUE)[-1] # calculating the SD propagated through the averaging equation
  # and we will select the greater of the two estimates of uncertainty
  src.SD <- pmax(src.SD.pop, src.SD.prop, na.rm=TRUE)
  ## Now we will simulate n samples for each organic matter source group
  nsams <- 10 # number of samples to simulate from each source
  Data_sources <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nsams*length(Sources), ncol = length(c(tracers,SDtracers))+1)) # initializing empty data frame
  colnames(Data_sources) <- c(tracers,SDtracers,Source_Variable) # naming columns
  for (i in variables) {
    Data_sources[[i]] <- seq(1,nsams*length(Sources))
  }
  Data_sources[[Source_Variable]] <- rep(Sources,nsams)
  Data_sources[[Source_Variable]] <- factor(Data_sources[[Source_Variable]], levels = Sources)
  set.seed(222) # set seed for repeatable sample synthesis
  for (i in 1:length(tracers)) { # loop over each tracer
    Data_sources[[tracers[i]]] <- 
      rnorm(n = nsams*length(Sources), # pulling n samples from a normal distribution
            mean = src.mn[[tracers[i]]], # with a given mean
            sd = src.SD[[SDtracers[i]]]) # and given SD
  }
  for (j in 1:length(Sources)) {
    Data_sources[which(Data_sources$Group == Sources[j]),SDtracers] <- 
      src.mn[j,SDtracers]
  }
}

# Calculating dN_SAA(Ser,Gly,Phe,Lys) and its SD
Data.all$SAA <-
  (Data.all$d15NPhe + Data.all$d15NGly + 
     Data.all$d15NSer + Data.all$d15NLys)/4
Data.all$SDSAA <- 
  sqrt(Data.all$SDd15NPhe^2 + Data.all$SDd15NGly^2 + 
         Data.all$SDd15NSer^2 + Data.all$SDd15NLys^2)/4

# Calculating dN_SAA(Gly,Phe,Lys) and its SD
Data.all$SAAB <-
  (Data.all$d15NPhe + Data.all$d15NGly + 
     Data.all$d15NLys)/3
Data.all$SDSAAB <- 
  sqrt(Data.all$SDd15NPhe^2 + Data.all$SDd15NGly^2 + 
         Data.all$SDd15NLys^2)/3

# Calculating dNSAA(Phe,Lys) and its SD
Data.all$SAAC <-
  (Data.all$d15NPhe + Data.all$d15NLys)/2
Data.all$SDSAAC <- 
  sqrt(Data.all$SDd15NPhe^2 + Data.all$SDd15NLys^2)/2

# Calculating dNSAA(Ser,Gly) and its SD
Data.all$SAAD <-
  (Data.all$d15NSer + Data.all$d15NGly)/2
Data.all$SDSAAD <- 
  sqrt(Data.all$SDd15NSer^2 + Data.all$SDd15NGly^2)/2

# turning any infinite SDs into 0.5s
Data.all$SDSAA[is.na(Data.all$SDSAA)] <- 0.5
Data.all$SDSAAB[is.na(Data.all$SDSAAB)] <- 0.5
Data.all$SDSAAC[is.na(Data.all$SDSAAC)] <- 0.5
Data.all$SDSAAD[is.na(Data.all$SDSAAD)] <- 0.5
Data.all$SDd15NPhe[is.na(Data.all$SDd15NPhe)] <- 0.5


# Calculating TP_Glx-Phe with SD
Data.all$TPGlx <- (Data.all$d15NGlx - Data.all$d15NPhe - TP.parms$beta[1])/TP.parms$TDF[1]+1
Data.all$sdTPGlx <- (Data.all$SDd15NGlx/TP.parms$TDF[1])^2+
  (Data.all$SDd15NPhe/TP.parms$TDF[1])^2+
  (TP.parms$SDbeta[1]/TP.parms$TDF[1])^2+
  ((Data.all$d15NPhe + TP.parms$beta[1] - Data.all$d15NGlx)/
     TP.parms$TDF[1]^2)^2 * TP.parms$SDTDF[1]^2

# Calculating TP_Ala-Phe with SD
Data.all$TPAla <- (Data.all$d15NAla - Data.all$d15NPhe - TP.parms$beta[2])/TP.parms$TDF[2]+1
Data.all$sdTPAla <- (Data.all$SDd15NAla/TP.parms$TDF[2])^2+
  (Data.all$SDd15NPhe/TP.parms$TDF[2])^2+
  (TP.parms$SDbeta[2]/TP.parms$TDF[2])^2+
  ((Data.all$d15NPhe + TP.parms$beta[2] - Data.all$d15NAla)/
     TP.parms$TDF[2]^2)^2 * TP.parms$SDTDF[2]^2

# Calculating dTP with SD
Data.all$dTP <- Data.all$TPAla-Data.all$TPGlx
Data.all$sddTP <- sqrt(Data.all$TPAla^2+Data.all$TPGlx^2)

\(\mathrm{\delta^{15}\text{N}_\text{SAA}}\) Values Across Depth

Now let’s generate some plots of \(\mathrm{\delta^{15}\text{N}_\text{SAA}}\) values in particles and zooplankton across depth. We’ll be comparing values in Epoch 1 vs Epoch 3 and considering an average of all 4 SAAs (Ser, Gly, Phe, and Lys) as well as only Phe and Lys.

Nighttime zooplankton \(\mathrm{\delta^{15}\text{N}_\text{SAA}}\) values relative to particles

Data.plots.parts = subset(Data.all,
                          (Sizecat == 'Large' | Sizecat=='Small'|Sizecat=='Submicron'))

Data.plots.zoops = subset(Data.all,
                          Type == "Consumer" & Event == "Night" & Sizecat=="Mixed")
Data.plots.zoops$Epoch[Data.plots.zoops$Epoch == 1] <- "Epoch 1"
Data.plots.zoops$Epoch[Data.plots.zoops$Epoch == 3] <- "Epoch 3"

Data.plots.zoops.day = subset(Data.all,
                          Type == "Consumer" & Event == "Day" & Sizecat=="Mixed")
Data.plots.zoops.day$Epoch[Data.plots.zoops.day$Epoch == 1] <- "Epoch 1"
Data.plots.zoops.day$Epoch[Data.plots.zoops.day$Epoch == 3] <- "Epoch 3"

Data.plots.zoops.taxa = subset(Data.all,
                          Type == "Consumer" & Sizecat!="Mixed")

Data.plots.traps = subset(Data.all, Sizecat=="Trap")

## Now were going to average particle d15N SAA data within depth bins so it can be compared with zoops (bins: 1-50, 51-100, 101-150, 151-200, 201-300, 301-400, 401-500)
maxdepths = c(50,100,150,200,300,400,500,750,1000)
mindepths = c(1,51,101,151,201,301,401,501,751,1001)
avgdepths = c(25,75,125,175,250,350,450,625,875)
DF = Data.plots.parts[,c('Depth','Type','SAA','SDSAA','SAAB','SDSAAB','SAAC','SDSAAC','SAAD','SDSAAD','SAAD','SDSAAD',"d15NPhe","SDd15NPhe")]
## Computing the average for all SAAs
Data.avg.parts = Data.plots.parts %>%
  group_by(Depth = cut(DF$Depth, breaks=mindepths, labels = avgdepths), Sizecat, Location, .drop = TRUE) %>%
  summarise(SDSAA = max(sd(SAA, na.rm = TRUE),SDmean(SDSAA), na.rm = TRUE),
            SAA = mean(SAA, na.rm = TRUE), # All SAAs
            SDSAAB = max(sd(SAAB, na.rm = TRUE),SDmean(SDSAAB), na.rm = TRUE),
            SAAB = mean(SAAB, na.rm = TRUE), # Gly, Phe, Lys 
            SDSAAC = max(sd(SAAC, na.rm = TRUE),SDmean(SDSAAC), na.rm = TRUE), 
            SAAC = mean(SAAC, na.rm = TRUE), # Phe, Lys
            SDSAAD = max(sd(SAAD, na.rm = TRUE),SDmean(SDSAAD), na.rm = TRUE),
            SAAD = mean(SAAD, na.rm = TRUE), # Phe, Lys 
            SDd15NPhe = max(sd(d15NPhe, na.rm = TRUE),SDmean(SDd15NPhe), na.rm = TRUE),
            d15NPhe = mean(d15NPhe, na.rm = TRUE),
            SDd15NLys = max(sd(d15NLys, na.rm = TRUE),SDmean(SDd15NLys), na.rm = TRUE),
            d15NLys = mean(d15NLys, na.rm = TRUE)) # Lys
   `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Depth', 'Sizecat'. You can override using
   the `.groups` argument.
Data.avg.parts$Depth = as.numeric(as.character(Data.avg.parts$Depth))

Data.plots.avg.parts <- Data.avg.parts
## Plotting all SAAs
plot.parts_zoops.SAA =
  ggplot(data = Data.plots.avg.parts[!is.na(Data.plots.avg.parts$SAA),],
         aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, xmin = SAA-SDSAA, xmax = SAA+SDSAA, color = Sizecat, group = Sizecat))+
  scale_y_reverse() + 
  geom_path(size = 1, alpha=0.8)+
  geom_ribbon(aes(fill = Sizecat), alpha = 0.50, color = NA)+labs(fill = "Particles")+
  geom_errorbarh(data = Data.plots.zoops,
                 aes(x = SAA, xmin = SAA-SDSAA, xmax = SAA+SDSAA), height = 1, size = 0.5, linetype = 1, color = 'black')+
  geom_point(data = Data.plots.zoops,
             aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, xmin = SAA-SDSAA, xmax = SAA+SDSAA, shape = Epoch),
             color = "black", fill="black", stroke = 0.5, size=3)+
  geom_path(data = Data.plots.zoops,
            aes(x = SAA, xmin = SAA-SDSAA, xmax = SAA+SDSAA, y = Depth, group = interaction(Location,Epoch)),
            color = "black", size = 1, linetype = "solid", alpha = 0.20)+
  geom_point(data = Data.plots.traps,
             aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, xmin = SAA-SDSAA, xmax = SAA+SDSAA, size=""),
             color = "black", stroke = 1, shape = 6)+
  geom_errorbarh(data = Data.plots.traps,
                 aes(x = SAA, xmin = SAA-SDSAA, xmax = SAA+SDSAA), height = 1, size = 0.5, linetype = 1, color = 'black')+
  # geom_point(data = Data.plots.zoops.day,
  #            aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, xmin = SAA-SDSAA, xmax = SAA+SDSAA, 
  #                shape = Epoch), 
             # color="black", fill=NA, size=2, stroke=1)+
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21,24))+
  scale_size_manual(values = c(2))+
  labs(color = "Particles", size = "Sediment Traps", shape = "Zooplankton")+
  xlab(expression(delta^{15}*"N"["Ser, Gly, Phe, Lys"]*" (\u2030)")) + ylab('Depth (m)') + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim=c(750,0), xlim=c(-6,6))+
  scale_x_continuous(minor_breaks = seq(-6,10,2), breaks = seq(-6,10,2))+
  scale_shape_discrete(labels = c("May 6","May 26"))+
  theme_light()+theme(text=element_text(family="serif"))+
  facet_wrap(~ Size, nrow = 1)+
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        text=element_text(family="serif"))
   Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
   ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
   This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
   Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
   generated.

   Ignoring unknown aesthetics: x

   SAA - : Ignoring unknown aesthetics: xmin and xmax

   Ignoring unknown aesthetics: xmin and xmax
   Warning in geom_point(data = Data.plots.traps, aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, xmin =
   SAA - : Ignoring unknown aesthetics: xmin and xmax

   Ignoring unknown aesthetics: x

plot.parts_zoops.SAA
   Warning: Using the `size` aesthetic with geom_path was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
   ℹ Please use the `linewidth` aesthetic instead.
   This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
   Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
   generated.



## Plotting Phe and Lys
plot.parts_zoops.SAAC =
  ggplot(data = Data.plots.avg.parts[!is.na(Data.plots.avg.parts$SAA),],
         aes(x = SAAC, y = Depth, xmin = SAAC-SDSAAC, xmax = SAAC+SDSAAC, color = Sizecat, group = Sizecat))+
  scale_y_reverse() + 
  geom_path(size = 1, alpha=0.8)+
  geom_ribbon(aes(fill = Sizecat), alpha = 0.50, color = NA)+labs(fill = "Particles")+
  geom_errorbarh(data = Data.plots.zoops,
                 aes(x = SAAC, xmin = SAAC-SDSAAC, xmax = SAAC+SDSAAC), height = 1, size = 0.5, linetype = 1, color = 'black')+
  geom_point(data = Data.plots.zoops,
             aes(x = SAAC, y = Depth, xmin = SAAC-SDSAAC, xmax = SAAC+SDSAAC, shape = Epoch),
             color = "black", fill="black", stroke = 0.5, size=3)+
  geom_path(data = Data.plots.zoops,
            aes(x = SAAC, xmin = SAAC-SDSAAC, xmax = SAAC+SDSAAC, y = Depth, group = interaction(Location,Epoch)),
            color = "black", size = 1, linetype = "solid", alpha = 0.20)+
  geom_point(data = Data.plots.traps,
             aes(x = SAAC, y = Depth, xmin = SAAC-SDSAAC, xmax = SAAC+SDSAAC, size=""),
             color = "black", stroke = 1, shape = 6)+
  geom_errorbarh(data = Data.plots.traps,
                 aes(x = SAAC, xmin = SAAC-SDSAAC, xmax = SAAC+SDSAAC), height = 1, size = 0.5, linetype = 1, color = 'black')+
  # geom_point(data = Data.plots.zoops.day,
  #            aes(shape = Epoch), 
  #            color="black", fill=NA, size=2, stroke=1)+
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21,24))+
  scale_size_manual(values = c(2))+
  labs(color = "Particles", size = "Sediment Traps", shape = "Zooplankton")+
  xlab(expression(delta^{15}*"N"["Phe, Lys"]*" (\u2030)")) + ylab('Depth (m)') + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim=c(750,0), xlim=c(-6,6))+
  scale_x_continuous(minor_breaks = seq(-6,10,2), breaks = seq(-6,10,2))+
  scale_shape_discrete(labels = c("May 6","May 26"))+
  theme_light()+theme(text=element_text(family="serif"))+
  facet_wrap(~ Size, nrow = 1)+
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        text=element_text(family="serif"))

# plot.parts_zoops.Lys

Differences in \(\mathrm{\delta^{15}\text{N}_\text{SAA}}\) values between epochs are modest for most zooplanton size classes, though zooplankton generally had lower values in Epoch 1. The smallest zooplankton appear to have lower values in epoch 1 than epoch 3, specifically by about 2‰ in the mesopelagic. This is could indicate increased reliance on surface POM early in the cruise, or reflect increasing \(\mathrm{\delta^{15}\text{N}}\) values in nitrate over the month of April combined with low growth rate in mesopelagic zooplankton.

Nighttime zooplankton TP relative to particles

Next we’ll plot TP of zooplankton relative to particles across depth. We’ll consider both TP(Glx-Phe) and TP(Ala-Phe) though we will primarily consider TP(Ala-Phe) because it reflect both proto- and metazoan trophic steps.

## Plotting TP(Glx-Phe)
plot.parts_zoops.TPGlx =
  ggplot()+
  scale_y_reverse() + 
  geom_point(
    data = Data.plots.parts[-which(colnames(Data.plots.parts)=="Size")],
    aes(x = TPGlx, y = Depth, 
        xmin = TPGlx-sdTPGlx, xmax = TPGlx+sdTPGlx, 
        color = Sizecat), 
    position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.height = 10),
    shape = 16, size=1.5, stroke=1)+
  geom_point(
    data = Data.plots.traps,
    aes(x = TPGlx, y = Depth, 
        xmin = TPGlx-sdTPGlx, xmax = TPGlx+sdTPGlx, 
        size=""),
    color = "black", stroke = 0.75, shape = 6)+
  geom_errorbarh(
    data = Data.plots.zoops,
    aes(x = TPGlx, y = Depth, 
        xmin = TPGlx-sdTPGlx, xmax = TPGlx+sdTPGlx), 
    height = 1, size = 0.5, linetype = 1, color = 'black')+
  geom_point(
    data = Data.plots.zoops,
    aes(x = TPGlx, y = Depth, 
        xmin = TPGlx-sdTPGlx, xmax = TPGlx+sdTPGlx, 
        shape = Epoch),
    color = "black", fill="black", stroke = 0.5, size=3)+
  geom_path(
    data = Data.plots.zoops,
    aes(x = TPGlx, y = Depth,
        xmin = TPGlx-sdTPGlx, xmax = TPGlx+sdTPGlx, 
        group = interaction(Location,Epoch)),
    color = "black", size = 1, linetype = "solid", alpha = 0.20)+
  facet_wrap(~ Size, nrow=1)+
  labs(color = "Particles", size = "Traps")

   == : Ignoring unknown aesthetics: xmin and xmax

   Ignoring unknown aesthetics: x

## Plotting TP(Glx-Phe)
plot.parts_zoops.TPAla =
  ggplot()+
  scale_y_reverse() + 
  geom_point(
    data = Data.plots.parts[-which(colnames(Data.plots.parts)=="Size")],
    aes(x = TPAla, y = Depth, 
        xmin = TPAla-sdTPAla, xmax = TPAla+sdTPAla, 
        color = Sizecat), 
    position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.height = 10),
    shape = 16, size=1.5, stroke=1)+
  geom_point(
    data = Data.plots.traps,
    aes(x = TPAla, y = Depth, 
        xmin = TPAla-sdTPAla, xmax = TPAla+sdTPAla, 
        size=""),
    color = "black", stroke = 0.75, shape = 6)+
  geom_errorbarh(
    data = Data.plots.zoops,
    aes(x = TPAla, y = Depth, 
        xmin = TPAla-sdTPAla, xmax = TPAla+sdTPAla), 
    height = 1, size = 0.5, linetype = 1, color = 'black')+
  geom_point(
    data = Data.plots.zoops,
    aes(x = TPAla, y = Depth, 
        xmin = TPAla-sdTPAla, xmax = TPAla+sdTPAla, 
        shape = Epoch),
    color = "black", fill="black", stroke = 0.5, size=3)+
  geom_path(
    data = Data.plots.zoops,
    aes(x = TPAla, y = Depth,
        xmin = TPAla-sdTPAla, xmax = TPAla+sdTPAla, 
        group = interaction(Location,Epoch)),
    color = "black", size = 1, linetype = "solid", alpha = 0.20)+
  facet_wrap(~ Size, nrow=1)+
  labs(color = "Particles", size = "Traps")

Zooplankton were consistently TP = 3-4, with little systematic change between epochs. We do notice that >5 mm zooplankton had higher TP in Epoch 3, likely due to the arrival of large hypriid amphipods at that time.
